message:
feat(fcp-host,gcal): stamp canonical CapabilityGrants + flatten gcal.* capability ids

Two related pieces of the capability-id rework land here. No public
host API is removed; the new shape is strictly more expressive.

connectors/google-calendar/src/connector.rs: flatten the per-operation
declared capability ids so the connector matches the manifest's new
shape (the per-operation rename landed in manifest and test commits
earlier in this push):

  gcal.calendars.list           gcal.calendars.read  -> gcal.read
  gcal.get_event                gcal.events.read     -> gcal.read
  gcal.list_events              gcal.events.read     -> gcal.read
  gcal.create_event             gcal.events.write    -> gcal.write
  gcal.update_event             gcal.events.write    -> gcal.write
  gcal.delete_event             gcal.events.write    -> gcal.write

The operation ids themselves (gcal.get_event, gcal.list_events, etc.)
are unchanged; only the `capability` field each operation declares to
the introspection surface is rewritten.

crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs: evolve
`CapabilityIssuanceRequest` + token minting so issuance stamps
canonical `CapabilityGrant`s pairing a capability with each operation.

- Add `capability_id: String` to `CapabilityIssuanceRequest`. The live
  invoke verifier checks the operation's declared capability, not
  just the operation id, so issuance must commit to which capability
  is being granted (previously only the operation list was recorded).
- Replace the `capability_constraints_from_request` "maybe-construct"
  helper with one that always returns a `CapabilityConstraints`,
  defaulting `resource_allow` to `["*"]` when the caller left it
  empty. The prior `Option<...>` was indistinguishable from "no
  constraint" on the wire and forced downstream verifiers to treat
  the absence of a constraint block as allow-everything implicitly.
- New `capability_grants_from_request` builds a
  `Vec<CapabilityGrant>` from the request. If `operations` is empty,
  a single grant with `operation: None` is emitted (coarse grant). If
  operations are present, one grant per operation is emitted, each
  pairing the supplied `capability_id` with the canonical
  `OperationId`. Both the capability and each operation are parsed
  through their canonical-id validators; parse failures surface as
  `LifecycleError::Persistence { reason: "invalid capability issuance
  request: ..." }` so the admin error path is uniform.
- Import adds `CapabilityGrant, CapabilityId, OperationId` to the
  `fcp_core::{...}` use-list.

crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs: update the three test call sites
that build a `CapabilityIssuanceRequest { connector_id, zone_id,
principal_id, operations: vec!["test.echo"], ... }` to also pass
`capability_id: "cap.test.echo".to_string()` so the tests compile
against the new request shape.

files.patch:
@@ -5647,6 +5647,7 @@ mod tests {
             .issue_capability_token(
                 &fcp_host::CapabilityIssuanceRequest {
                     connector_id: connector_id.to_string(),
+                    capability_id: "cap.test.echo".to_string(),
                     zone_id: ZoneId::work().to_string(),
                     principal_id: "user:test".to_string(),
                     operations: vec!["test.echo".to_string()],
@@ -6061,6 +6062,7 @@ mod tests {
             .issue_capability_token(
                 &fcp_host::CapabilityIssuanceRequest {
                     connector_id: connector_id.to_string(),
+                    capability_id: "cap.test.echo".to_string(),
                     zone_id: ZoneId::work().to_string(),
                     principal_id: "user:test".to_string(),
                     operations: vec!["test.echo".to_string()],
@@ -6161,6 +6163,7 @@ mod tests {
             .issue_capability_token(
                 &fcp_host::CapabilityIssuanceRequest {
                     connector_id: connector_id.to_string(),
+                    capability_id: "cap.test.echo".to_string(),
                     zone_id: ZoneId::work().to_string(),
                     principal_id: "user:test".to_string(),
                     operations: vec!["test.echo".to_string()],
